output.summary_final:
{"title": "Trail Maintenance & Riding Tips", "bullets": [
"Focus on braking earlier and smoother for better speed control.",
"Regular bike maintenance, including cleaning contact surfaces and re-greasing threads, is crucial for a quiet and predictable ride.",
"A good trail drains well, staying rideable longer and preventing ruts.",
"For climbs, maintain a consistent cadence to reduce drivetrain load and manage traction changes.",
"When conditions are wet, prioritize decision-making and know when to back off to protect traction and trails.",
"Practice on one trail segment at a time, changing only one variable to improve faster."
]}

{"title": "On-Call That Doesn't Break People", "bullets": [
"Good on-call rotations minimize disruptions, ensuring responses are clear and obvious when incidents occur.",
"Secrets management is strengthened through short-lived tokens, narrow scopes, and automated rotation.",
"Healthy systems anticipate failures with timeouts, retries, and clear ownership responsibilities.",
"Threat modeling should be straightforward, focusing on assets, entry points, trust boundaries, and potential abuse cases.",
"Well-structured RFCs provide context, constraints, options, recommendations, and explicit risks.",
"Architecture decisions often involve tradeoffs between cost, reliability, speed, correctness, autonomy, and consistency." ,
"Alerting should be action-oriented; if immediate action isn't possible, avoid unnecessary paging."
]}

{"title": "Wreck Diving Fundamentals", "bullets": [
"Calmness is crucial for safe diving, as it promotes slow breathing and deliberate decisions.",
"Choose one primary and one backup navigation reference (e.g., heading plus depth or reef contour plus current direction).",
"A backup light provides redundancy and a mental safety net by offering options in emergencies.",
"Practice regulator free-flow procedures calmly to handle potential issues effectively.",
"Good buoyancy control protects the environment, reduces task load for navigation and photography, and prevents silt disturbance.",
"Neutral buoyancy drills (hovering and slow fin pivots) enhance overall diving skills and make navigating choppy conditions easier.",
"Dive plans should be simple, shared, and include clear turn pressures and exit routes."
]}

{"title": "Underwater Photography Tips", "bullets": [
"A simple setup executed consistently is key to great underwater images.",
"Adjusting strobe angle can reduce backscatter dramatically.",
"Labeling files and writing captions the same day prevents memory gaps in long-term projects.",
"One-light practice forces you to learn placement and understand lighting fundamentals.",
"In mixed lighting, decide on a dominant color temperature and adjust everything else accordingly.",
"Underwater photography is primarily about lighting and positioning; the camera is rarely the limiting factor."
]}

{"title": "Index Investing Without Myths", "bullets": [
"Risk is not volatility; it's the chance of not meeting your goals or suffering permanent loss.",
"Position sizing rules prevent one great idea from becoming a portfolio-ending mistake.",
"Fees compound quietly, lowering costs is like increasing returns without taking additional risk.",
"The hardest part of investing is staying consistent when headlines are loud and portfolios are down.",
"Diversification should include different drivers, not just different tickers that move together.",
"A simple stress test can help you determine if you can afford higher rates, longer vacancies, and unexpected repairs without panic selling."
]}
